pages.raw_text:
The chief interest of the morning
Boone of New York, the present edl-
tor of "Education," and formerly SUI
perintendent Of Cincinnati, Ohio,
schools and president of the Michigan
State Normal college.


DR.. Boone's subject was "The Book
Habit" and his talk was of especial
value to the teachers. The eminent
doctor is one of those rarities in the
teaching professional man who can
make good talk on any educational
subject.


He said in part: "The eating of
cooked food is habit that had to be
acquired just as the wearing of clothes
or the habit of ladies in keeping their
hats on at public meeting. Civilizing
processes are always hard on the civ
ilized. A recent attempt to civilize d
certain barbarous island people re-
suited in the determination of S. per
cent of the population. The eating Of
cooked food came relatively late in
the history of the race. Probably the
wearing of clothing came still later.
Likewise the development of the book
publishing business has been gradual,
and it is only within the last three
hundred years or so that man has come
to depend upon books as he certainly
did not in the time of the papyrus roll
or the waxed tablet.


"Only three out of the twenty-four
barons who brought forth the Magma
Charta could sign their names. The
others had to make their mark. They
were not dependent upon books in
those days.


Nowadays books are regarded much
as mans clothing or his three meals
day-as indispensable.


children should be taught the use
of certain reference books in the third
and fourth grades. To use references
properly is the desideratum.


mo other instrument is SO potent
for getting information before the age
of ten as books. A child can get
more from them in the same time than
from nature"
